_note: Model registry — edit 'enabled' to toggle models, 'default_model' to set the default.

auto.description: Classifies each task (recon, exploit, code, etc.) and routes to the best-performing model based on historical scores. Recommended for autonomous operation.

w12.description: Fast general-purpose model. Used as default for speed-critical operations and as primary fallback.

w13.description: Strong reasoning and analysis. Used for complex exploitation chains and strategic decisions.

w480b.description: Largest available model for deep code review, payload generation, and complex vulnerability analysis. Slower but more thorough.

m3.description: Lightweight model for quick local tasks. Runs via Ollama. Best for offline use or when API keys are unavailable.

kimi.resolves_to: Kimi model (prompts sanitized for target IPs/domains)
kimi.description: Safety-filtered model — prompts are sanitized to remove IPs/domains before sending to avoid refusals.

gemma4.description: Safety-filtered model — prompts are sanitized to remove IPs/domains before sending.

default.description: Standard penetration testing persona. Balances speed and thoroughness.

redteam.description: Aggressive red-team persona. Prioritizes exploitation speed and evasion.

blackhat.description: Simulated adversary persona with no constraints on technique selection.

phase_model_overrides._note: Override model for specific engagement phases. Empty string = use default_model.
